title: Reschedule Politely
summary: Ask to change a planned meeting or appointment without causing inconvenience.
learnerPromise: You will be able to ask to reschedule in a way that respects the other person's time and keeps the relationship positive.
whyItMatters: Plans change sometimes. Knowing how to reschedule politely protects your relationships and makes it more likely the other person will say yes.
realLifeUses: Moving a meeting with a classmate to study together
Asking a friend to meet on a different day
Shifting a video call with a colleague to another time slot
result: A polite message sent asking to reschedule, with an alternative time offered
youWillLearn: Notify the other person as soon as you know you need to change plans
Offer one or two alternative times that work for you
Confirm the new time once it is agreed on
miniChallenge: Send a message to someone you have plans with, asking to reschedule and offering two other times that work for you.
steps: Check your calendar for two or three times you are free
Write a message saying you need to change the plan and briefly apologize
Send the message as soon as possible
Once they reply, confirm the new time with a short message
completionCriteria: You sent the reschedule request before the original meeting time
Your message includes an apology and at least one alternative time
A new time is confirmed by both sides
commonProblems: Waiting until the last minute to ask for a change
Offering only one alternative time, which may not work for the other person
Forgetting to confirm the new plan once it is discussed
tips: The earlier you ask to reschedule, the more flexible the other person can be
Offering two or three options makes it easier for them to say yes
Keep the apology short and sincere; long explanations can sound like excuses
